13aMiu Miu · Personalisation
Custom Studio
Akeneo · Configurator flow
Personalisation dataMTO logicPDP configurator
View live product ↗
What it does
A PDP configurator that brings Miu Miu's Made to Order experience to e-commerce — customers personalise selected pieces directly on the product page, with every customisation option and SKU eligibility rule structured end-to-end in Akeneo.
Try it Demo
Miu Miu
Sneakers Gymnasium
in mesh and suede
€ 870
COLOR: Desert
SELECT SIZE (IT): Size chart
Find in store Product details Shipping & Returns
−
Remove
Totale € 870
1 Prodotto
Deep dive
Approach
Personalisation options are configured as attributes in Akeneo, linked to qualifying SKUs. SAP Commerce Cloud reads those attributes to build the PDP configurator — guiding the customer through available choices before add-to-cart and order placement.
Stack
Akeneo (personalisation attribute configuration and SKU eligibility), SAP Commerce Cloud (MTO configurator and order flow), Operations (Made to Order production coordination and fulfilment).
Impact
Miu Miu's bespoke Made to Order experience — previously available only in-store — is now accessible online, reaching a broader audience without compromising the craftsmanship positioning.
My role
Configured personalisation attributes in Akeneo — defining available choices per product, setting SKU eligibility rules, and maintaining data accuracy across the full range.
Validated the end-to-end customisation flow across all qualifying SKUs, identifying and resolving attribute gaps that blocked configurator activation before launch.
Collaborators
Development — SAP CC configurator build, MTO order flow integration, and PDP rendering.
Operations — Made to Order production handoff, fulfilment coordination, and customer communication.

Deep dive
Approach
Customers select material (Pelle / Vernice), colour, and personalisation options through a SAP Commerce Cloud MTO configurator. Each choice maps to a dedicated attribute in Akeneo — giving full data control over available configurations per SKU and seasonal range.
Stack
Akeneo (personalisation attribute schema and MTO configuration management), SAP Commerce Cloud (3D configurator and Made to Order order flow), AC Factory Operations (production handoff and quality coordination).
Impact
AC Factory's artisanal personalisation — previously in-store only — now available online. MTO attribution in Akeneo keeps production data accurate from configuration through delivery.

What it does
An AI widget on Prada and Church's PDPs that delivers personalised size recommendations at the moment of purchase — reducing size uncertainty and attributed to a +52% conversion uplift quarter-on-quarter.
Try it Demo
Cotton Jacket
€ 1.750

Clear data
Deep dive
Approach
Measmerize's AI engine maps product size and fit attributes from Akeneo to user input on the PDP. The widget activates at point-of-purchase, pulling accurate size logic per SKU and category from the enriched catalogue.
Stack
Measmerize AI (recommendation engine and ML model), Akeneo (size and fit attribute source), SAP Commerce Cloud (PDP widget integration), Google Analytics (conversion measurement and QoQ attribution).
Results
+52% conversion uplift QoQ across Prada and Church's PDPs. Improved size confidence at point-of-purchase reduces return rates and increases order completion.
My role
Enriched size and fit attributes in Akeneo across the Prada and Church's catalogues — ensuring the data feeding the Measmerize engine was accurate, complete, and maintained per SKU and category.
Tracked conversion performance via Google Analytics on a quarterly basis, flagging optimisation opportunities to the vendor and UX teams based on the uplift data.
Collaborators
Measmerize — AI recommendation engine, ML model configuration, and widget build.
Development & UX — PDP widget integration, placement, and interaction design.

05Prada · Eyewear UX
Virtual Try-On for Eyewear
Try-on engine · Akeneo tags · PDP widget
Computer visionAkeneo tagsPDP widget
View live product ↗
What it does
A live AR try-on on the Prada eyewear PDP — customers preview frames on their face via device camera, removing the fit uncertainty that makes eyewear one of the highest-hesitation categories in fashion e-commerce.
Try it Demo
Virtual Try-On
Prada Symbole sunglasses
€ 390
Color Slate Gray Lenses
Find in store Product details Free shipping and returns
Camera access required.
Allow access in your browser to try on.
Deep dive
Approach
Eyewear SKUs are tagged in Akeneo with eligibility attributes that activate the try-on widget. The computer vision engine reads those tags, accesses the device camera, and overlays real-time frame renders on a live video feed — all within the PDP.
Stack
Computer vision vendor (AR rendering engine and real-time overlay), Akeneo (product eligibility tagging and category attribute management), SAP Commerce Cloud (PDP widget integration and activation logic).
Impact
The Prada eyewear PDP moves from static imagery to an interactive fitting experience — reducing return friction and raising engagement in a category where fit confidence is the main purchase barrier.
My role
Managed eligibility tagging in Akeneo — ensuring every qualifying eyewear SKU carried the correct category attributes to trigger the try-on widget on the PDP.
Validated widget activation across the full eyewear assortment post-launch, resolving attribute gaps that prevented correct display on specific SKUs.
Collaborators
Computer vision vendor — AR rendering engine, real-time frame overlay technology, and widget build.
Development — PDP integration, widget embedding, and activation logic.
← Portfolio Leonardo Novelli

02Prada · Shoppability
Notify Me
Fluent OMS · Akeneo · PDP CTA
AkeneoFluent OMSPDP CTA
View live product ↗
What it does
When a Prada product sells out, the PDP doesn't dead-end — the CTA transitions from Add to Cart to Notify Me, capturing demand intent and triggering an automated confirmation email. Registered customers re-enter the funnel when stock returns, for up to 4 weeks.
Try it Demo
Prada
Route large canvas and leather tote bag
€ 2,400
Colour: Forest Green
Get notified when back in stock
You're on the list
We'll notify when this item is back in stock. A Prada confirmation email has been sent.
Deep dive
Approach
A stock-out in Fluent OMS triggers an attribute state change in Akeneo, flipping the CTA flag on the PDP. SAP Commerce Cloud renders the Notify Me component; the CRM handles email capture, sends an automated confirmation with product details, and holds the registration for 4 weeks.
Stack
Fluent OMS (real-time stock signals), Akeneo (CTA state attributes and SKU flags), SAP Commerce Cloud (PDP CTA rendering), CRM (Notify Me capture and automated confirmation email).
Results
Out-of-stock PDPs retain demand rather than losing it. Registered intent re-enters the funnel on restock — a recovery channel that didn't exist before the initiative.
My role
Configured the Akeneo attribute logic that maps Fluent OMS stock flags to the CTA state on the PDP — the data layer that controls whether customers see Add to Cart or Notify Me.
Validated CTA state transitions across SKUs and markets during rollout, identifying and resolving attribute gaps flagged in testing before go-live.
Collaborators
OMS team — Fluent integration, real-time stock signal configuration, and state-change triggers.
Development — PDP dynamic CTA component build and rendering logic.
← Portfolio Leonardo Novelli

01Miu Miu · Conversion
Cross-Selling & Product Matching
Akeneo · Algolia · SAP CC
Cross-sell logicAttribute pairingRecommendation ranking
View live product ↗
What it does
A recommendation system on Miu Miu PDPs that surfaces complementary products at point-of-browse — driven by attribute-based pairing logic in Akeneo, ranked by Algolia, and measured at +74% cross-sell uplift month-on-month.
Try it Demo
Arcadie Leather Bag
€ 2.450
Color: Cognac
Size:
Mini
Regular
Large
Find in store Product details Shipping & Returns
Select charm
Select an item
Deep dive
Approach
Product pairs are structured in Akeneo through shared season, silhouette, and category attributes. The recommendation component is served on the PDP via SAP Commerce Cloud and ranked by Algolia — placement and pairing logic managed directly at the product data level.
Stack
Akeneo (attribute-based pairing logic), SAP Commerce Cloud (recommendation serving and PDP rendering), Algolia (ranking and relevance scoring), Google Analytics (performance attribution and MoM uplift measurement).
Results
+74% cross-sell uplift MoM measured via Google Analytics. Increased average order value across the Miu Miu e-commerce channel.
My role
Built and maintained the product pairing attribute logic in Akeneo — defining complementary relationships across categories and seasons, and iterating on pairings based on MoM performance signals.
Tracked cross-sell uplift via Google Analytics and surfaced optimisation opportunities to the development and merchandising teams.
Collaborators
Development — recommendation component build and SAP CC integration.
Merchandising — final pairing validation and editorial sequencing priorities.

RO·01Rick Owens · Digital Assets
Product Asset Display
Shopify · Media QA
Image sequencingPDP qualityCatalogue upkeep
View live page ↗
What it does
Complete asset ownership across the Rick Owens Shopify storefront — upload, sequencing, and ongoing quality audits across every PDP to ensure imagery is accurate, in the correct order, and brand-standard on every page.
Deep dive
Approach
Season assets arrive from the creative team and are uploaded to Shopify in correct sequence — hero shot, detail shots, editorial. Regular audits flag sequencing errors, missing files, or outdated imagery before they impact the live storefront.
Stack
Shopify (asset upload, media management, and PDP configuration), creative team deliverables (photography and retouching), internal QA process for image accuracy and sequencing consistency.
Impact
Correct, well-sequenced imagery is a direct conversion lever — customers see the right product in the right context in the right order. Consistent quality across all PDPs upholds the Rick Owens brand standard online.
My role
Uploaded and sequenced product assets for all new season arrivals and ongoing catalogue updates — hero, detail, and editorial shots in the correct order across every PDP.
Ran regular quality audits across the live catalogue, identifying and resolving asset errors, missing files, and sequencing inconsistencies.
Collaborators
Creative — photography, retouching, and seasonal image delivery.
Shopify development — storefront templates and PDP image component configuration.
← PortfolioLeonardo Novelli

RO·02Rick Owens · Editorial
FW24 Porterville Product Guide
Shopify · Story-driven guide
Collection narrativeShoppable editorialLaunch sequencing
View live page ↗
What it does
A Shopify editorial page built for the FW24 Porterville collection — collection photography and Vimeo video paired with product cards linked directly to live PDPs, turning brand storytelling into a shoppable journey.
Deep dive
Approach
Built as a Shopify page with editorial imagery, Vimeo-embedded video, and product cards mapped to in-stock PDPs. Product selection and sequencing were aligned against both the editorial narrative and live inventory — balancing creative direction with commercial availability.
Stack
Shopify (page build, product integration, asset embedding), Vimeo (video hosting and embed), creative team deliverables (editorial photography and video), e-commerce and creative team coordination.
Impact
Customers engaging with the Porterville collection narrative can move directly to live PDPs — closing the gap between brand discovery and purchase intent within a single editorial experience.
My role
Led product selection and sequencing for the guide — aligning editorial priorities with in-stock availability and ensuring commercial viability alongside creative intent.
Managed the Shopify page build end-to-end, coordinating asset integration between creative and e-commerce teams and ensuring both editorial and commercial requirements were met.
Collaborators
Creative — editorial art direction, photography, and Vimeo video production.
Shopify development — page template and editorial component build.
← PortfolioLeonardo Novelli
RO·03Rick Owens · CRM
Email Marketing CampaignsMC
Mailchimp · Audience segmentation
Campaign buildLifecycle CRMAudience logic
Private Sale ↗Converse x DRKSHDW ↗Last Week of Sale ↗
What it does
Three end-to-end Mailchimp campaigns for FW24 — Private Sale, Converse x DRKSHDW collaboration launch, and Last Week of Sale — each built from subject line to dispatch with audience segmentation, A/B tested copy, and performance tracking.
Deep dive
Approach
Each campaign was built in Mailchimp from template to dispatch — subject line, body copy, visual layout, and CTA placement. Audiences segmented by purchase history, engagement score, and geography. A/B testing on subject lines used to optimise open rate per segment.
Stack
Mailchimp (campaign build, audience management, and analytics), customer database segmented by purchase history, engagement score, and geographic region.
Results
Campaigns drove FW24 clearance sell-through and generated direct traffic to the Converse x DRKSHDW landing page. Open rate and CTR tracked above luxury fashion email benchmarks across all three sends.
My role
Owned all three campaigns end-to-end in Mailchimp — subject line writing, body copy, visual layout, CTA placement, mobile formatting, and final scheduling for each send.
Managed list segmentation for each campaign — splitting the database by purchase history, engagement score, and geography, and assigning the appropriate message variant per audience.
Collaborators
Commercial — promotional strategy, sale calendar, and discount structure per campaign.
Creative — email design templates and campaign imagery.
